stories.title: Bound auto-updater network calls with a 30-second timeout
Emit SHA256 sidecars for .deb, .rpm, and .AppImage release artifacts
Purge Cloudflare cache after R2 repo sync
stories.review_note: CONSIDER (not blocking): AC2 observable-property (`curl -I ... | grep cf-cache-status`) is verifiable post-release but no CI step asserts it. Risks table row #2 suggested a smoke-test step as defense-in-depth. Primary purge + retry-with-backoff correctly implemented.

stories.title: Pin CI workflow base image to ubuntu-22.04
Pin appimageupdatetool to a versioned tag with SHA256 verification
Clear stale review notes from the Linux packaging migration status file
Gate the legacy .run installer code path with #[cfg(unix)]
Wire macOS .dmg update install flow into self-update dispatch
stories.review_note: Hardware smoke-test (hdiutil + /Applications replace) still required on real macOS before first macOS release cut per Risks row #3. CONSIDER: switch `cp -R` at macos/dmg.rs:276 to `ditto --rsrc` (Apple-mandated for signed bundles with nested frameworks) — current PaneFlow.app is simple enough that cp -R works, but ditto future-proofs for richer bundle layouts.

stories.title: Wire Windows .msi update install flow into self-update dispatch
stories.review_note: Hardware smoke-test (msiexec UAC + exit codes) still required on Windows 11 before first Windows release cut per Risks row #3. CONSIDER: add explicit handling for msiexec exit 1618 (another install in progress — currently falls through to generic `Other` arm in map_exit_code at msi.rs:223-236).

stories.title: Dispatch xdg-open / open / explorer.exe per-OS in reveal-in-file-manager sites
stories.review_note: SHOULD_FIX before Windows release cut: workspace_ops/mod.rs:688-691 uses `.arg("/select,").arg(path)` which passes as two arguments — Windows Explorer expects `/select,<path>` concatenated in a single token. The comment at line 685-687 correctly states this invariant but the code violates it. Fix: `let mut flag = OsString::from("/select,"); flag.push(path.as_os_str()); Command::new("explorer").arg(flag).spawn()`. Also CONSIDER: sidebar/mod.rs:504-509 comment claims `open::that` is "byte-identical" to `cmd /C start ""` on Windows — actually uses ShellExecuteW. Functionally equivalent for https URLs; correct the comment wording to avoid misleading future maintainers. Neither blocks v0.2.0/v0.2.1; both should land before first Windows release.
